text.title: Structured Logging Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Structured Logging Patterns application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: Log lines are JSON objects with at minimum `level`, `timestamp`, `event`, `correlation_id`, `service`. Plain-text logs are forbidden in production.
rule.rationale: JSON allows structured queries in the aggregator; plain text forces grep + regex and loses fields.

rule.statement: Every log line within a request boundary carries the request's correlation_id. Middleware attaches it; child contexts inherit.
rule.rationale: Without correlation_id, tracing a multi-service request requires correlating timestamps and is unreliable.

rule.statement: Levels follow the contract: ERROR = actionable (page-able), WARN = degraded but recovering, INFO = significant state change, DEBUG = verbose / dev-only. No level inflation.
rule.rationale: Level inflation (everything is ERROR) trains operators to ignore signals; discipline preserves the alert path.

rule.statement: PII fields (email, phone, password, token, full name, address) are masked before logging. A single redaction pipeline applies; ad-hoc masking in call sites is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Single pipeline is the only auditable surface; per-call-site masking drifts and leaks.

rule.statement: Redaction happens in one processor / formatter / interceptor. The field list lives in code-as-config. PRs that change the redaction list require a paired ADR.
rule.rationale: Multiple redaction sites duplicate and drift; one source is the only reviewable shape.

rule.statement: print(), console.log(), fmt.Println in non-test paths is forbidden. Linting (T20 / no-console / forbidigo) enforces the rule.
rule.rationale: Stray prints escape redaction + correlation; lint enforcement is the cheap gate.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
